summary: Crisp sound‑board capture of the 1993 St. Patrick’s Day show with a blazing Shakedown, soaring Dark Star and magical Lucy in the Sky.
review: The recording is a generally clean sound‑board mix (with occasional hiss and a few channel swaps) that lets listeners hear the band’s high energy on this March ’93 run.  Highlights include an incendiary Shakedown opener, a monster Dark Star → Drums/Space, the crowd‑crazy Lucy in the Sky, plus memorable jams like Handsome Cabin Boy, Days Between and The Other One.  While some newer songs stumble and a few cuts (Eternity, Ramble On) are noted, most reviewers celebrate the performance as one of the era’s best.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews give detailed praise for both performance and sound; show quality dominates (≈70%) with many standout songs, while recording issues are minor enough to keep the overall rating in the good‑to‑great range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well balanced; a few reviewers note hiss, occasional channel reversal, and missing segments (Eternity, Ramble On).

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set starts strong with Shakedown but loses momentum after the new songs; the second set rebuilds, delivering a cohesive Dark Star → Drums/Space sequence followed by powerful jams that lift the show to a memorable peak.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring lead guitar on Dark Star, strong tone on Lazy River Road and energetic vocals (though a few note vocal strain on LSD).
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work described as solid and front‑center, especially in the second set where he “smiled a lot” and interplayed heavily.
band_member_comments.Bob: Rhythm guitar noted positively on Wang Dang Doodle and overall fun contributions.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboards called “tripy”, with tasteful chording during Days Between and strong presence throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill & Mickey receive consistent praise for a tight, dynamic drums/space segment.
